creator: vd@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello,

archivers/py-librtfcomp and graphics/py-ming seem to be the only ports
that set PLIST to an empty value which causes the generate-plist
target from bsd.port.mk to hang:

hag:/usr/ports/archivers/py-librtfcomp# make install
...
===>  Staging for py27-librtfcomp-1.3
===>   py27-librtfcomp-1.3 depends on file: /usr/local/bin/python2.7 - found
===>   Generating temporary packing list
 ^C

/usr/ports/archivers/py-librtfcomp# make generate-plist
===>   Generating temporary packing list
^C

I confirm that this patch to bsd.port.mk fixes the hang (thanks, Klaus Aehlig!):

--- cut ---
--- /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk.orig    2014-03-06 14:38:26.000000000 +0200
+++ /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk    2014-03-06 14:38:15.000000000 +0200
@@ -5553,7 +5553,7 @@
 .endif
 .endfor
 .endif
-    @if [ -f ${PLIST} ]; then \
+    @if [ -f "${PLIST}" ]; then \
         ${SED} ${PLIST_SUB:S/$/!g/:S/^/ -e s!%%/:S/=/%%!/} ${PLIST}
>> ${TMPPLIST}; \
     fi

--- cut ---

but maybe it should be fixed in the ports themselves too?

There are these two other ports that set PLIST to an "interesting" value:

/usr/ports/security/sinfp/Makefile:PLIST=        ""
/usr/ports/net-p2p/libtorrent-rasterbar-python/Makefile:PLIST=
${NONEXISTENT}

no hang for them.

Thank you!
